title: Las Vegas Sands (LVS) Intrinsic Value
description: An independent discounted cash flow (DCF) intrinsic value analysis of Las Vegas Sands (LVS).

fcfGrowth.title: FCF Growth Rate (Y1-Y5)
fcfGrowth.rationale: An 8% growth rate assumes continued normalization and steady growth in Macau's mass market, alongside robust contributions from the expanded Marina Bay Sands operations in Singapore.

discountRate.title: Discount Rate (WACC)
discountRate.rationale: A higher 10% discount rate is strictly applied to account for the significant regulatory risks in Macau and the company's concentrated exposure to the unpredictable Chinese macroeconomic environment.

terminalGrowth.title: Terminal Growth Rate
terminalGrowth.rationale: 2.5% reflects mature, long-term growth expectations for the global gaming and resort industry, assuming a stabilization of the Asian consumer market.

thesisParagraphs: Las Vegas Sands is a pure-play on the Asian gaming and integrated resort market. Having strategically divested its iconic Las Vegas properties, the company's future is entirely tethered to the economic vitality of Macau and Singapore. The business model relies heavily on the 'premium mass' segment, utilizing its massive 1.78B free cash flow generation to fund world-class retail and entertainment that drives gaming volume.

While the long-term demographic trends in Asia point toward rising consumer discretionary spending, the stock carries significant, undeniable geopolitical and regulatory risk. The valuation hinges not just on consumer demand, but on the continued stability of the Macau operating environment and the successful execution of multi-billion dollar expansion projects currently underway.
verdictFooter: Based on FY2025 actuals. Data via S&P Global (Mar 20, 2026).

faq.question: Why is the discount rate 10%?
faq.answer: The higher discount rate reflects the significant geographic concentration risk and the highly complex, unpredictable regulatory environment of operating casinos in Macau.

faq.question: What is the biggest potential catalyst?
faq.answer: Beyond general economic recovery, the successful acquisition of a new gaming license in a major untapped market like Thailand or New York would fundamentally shift the growth narrative.

faq.question: Does LVS still operate in Las Vegas?
faq.answer: No, Las Vegas Sands completed the sale of its remaining Las Vegas properties in 2022 to focus its capital and operations entirely on its higher-margin Asian resorts.
